person.name: Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT

memoryArtifacts.headline: Rogier de Beaufort D'argent à la bande d'azur accompagnée de six roses de gueules, trois en chef et trois en pointe.
memoryArtifacts.description:
Armoirie de la famille Rogier de Beaufort : 
D'argent à la bande d'azur accompagnée de six roses de gueules, trois en chef et trois en pointe.

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Armoirie de la famille Rogier de Beaufort : 
D'argent à la bande d'azur accompagnée de six roses de gueules, trois en chef et trois en pointe.
Comte de Beaufort en Anjou en 1342 et 1347. 
Seigneur de Rosiers d'Egleton (Corrèze) et de Maumont en 1333.
Sources :
- Dictionnaire général d'Auvergne par A de Remacle pages 238/241.
-HG N° 135 p. 179/80 
-N° 165 p. 392 Copie ; 
- N° 134 p 170 ; 
-N° 186 p 92 Pestels ;
- Dictionnaire de la noblesse par La Chesnay Desboy pages 624 et suivantes.
- CHGP (Asso Périgord) N° 64 p.67.
- généanet Paul de Lapeyre ;
relevé JPR Teyssier Chalancon Manga

zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT於1265年1月8日出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰可雷洛希艾斯-達艾格萊頓斯時，他的父親Pierre Rogier de Beaufort25歲，母親Nadale de Maulmont20歲。他於1288年與Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德博福特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1338年6月23日在他的家鄉享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: ROGIER
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Herve, Alain.
French: variant of   Roger  .

variants.name: Roger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: BEAUFORT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French   Beaufort  .
English (of Norman origin): variant of Beaufort . The surname Beauford is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beuford
meanings: Variant of   Beauford  .

variants.name: Beaufort
meanings: Altered form of French   Beaufort  .
English (of Norman origin): variant of Beaufort . The surname Beauford is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT was born on 8 January 1265, in Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, his father, Pierre Rogier de Beaufort, was 25 and his mother, Nadale de Maulmont, was 20. He married Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie in 1288, in Beaufort, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 23 June 1338, in his hometown, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT est né le 8 janvier 1265, à Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Pierre Rogier de Beaufort, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Nadale de Maulmont, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie en 1288, à Beaufort, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 23 juin 1338, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT am 8. Januar 1265 in Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Pierre Rogier de Beaufort 25 und seine Mutter Nadale de Maulmont 20. Er hat 1288 in Beaufort, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Juni 1338 im Alter von 73 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT è nato il il 8 gennaio 1265 nel Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, il suo padre, Pierre Rogier de Beaufort, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Nadale de Maulmont, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie nel 1288, nel Beaufort, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 23 giugno 1338, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORTが1265年1月8日にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインコルeズロシエルス-ド'eグレトンスで生まれた時，父Pierre Rogier de Beaufortは25歳，母Nadale de Maulmontは20歳であった。1288年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスノードビーウフォートでGuillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1338年6月23日に故郷で73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT(이)가 1265년 1월 8일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 코e즈 로시어스-드'e글레토스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Pierre Rogier de Beaufort(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Nadale de Maulmont(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1288년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 보포트에서 Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1338년 6월 23일 그의 고향에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.8 janvier 1265–23 juin 1338: 1265 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 8–1338 оны Зургаадугаар сарын 23

pt.8 janvier 1265–23 juin 1338: 8 de janeiro de 1265–23 de junho de 1338
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT nasceu em 8 de janeiro de 1265, em Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Pierre Rogier de Beaufort, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Nadale de Maulmont, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie em 1288, em Beaufort, Nord, Hauts-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de junho de 1338, em sua cidade natal, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT родился 8 января 1265 г. в местности росиирс-д'eглетонс, коррeз, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, его отцу по имени Pierre Rogier de Beaufort было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Nadale de Maulmont, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie в 1288 г. в местности бофорт, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер 23 июня 1338 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 73 лет.

es.8 janvier 1265–23 juin 1338: 8 de enero de 1265–23 de junio de 1338
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillaume Ier ROGIER de BEAUFORT nació el 8 de enero de 1265, en Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Pierre Rogier de Beaufort, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Nadale de Maulmont, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Guillemette de La Mestre De Tinière de Chapoulie en 1288, en Beaufort, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 23 de junio de 1338, en su pueblo, a la edad de 73 años.
